defs: (past tense marker used before verb or clause)
Zeng Shen (505-435 BC), a.k.a. 曾子[Zeng1 zi3], student of Confucius, presumed editor or author of Confucian classic the Great Learning 大學|大学[Da4 xue2]
Zeng Guofan (1811-1872), Qing dynasty politician and military man
Zengzi (505-435 BC), student of Confucius, presumed editor or author of Confucian classic the Great Learning 大學|大学[Da4 xue2]
Zeng Xiaogu (1873-1937), actor and pioneer of Chinese drama in New Culture style
Cang Jize or Tseng Chi-tse (1839-1890), pioneer diplomat of late Qing, serve as imperial commissioner (ambassador) to UK, France and Russia
(past tense marker used before verb or clause)
lit. having crossed the vast ocean (idiom)
fig. widely experienced in the vicissitudes of life

trad: 曾經滄海難為水，除卻巫山不是雲
simp: 曾经沧海难为水，除却巫山不是云
pinyin: ceng2 jing1 cang1 hai3 nan2 wei2 shui3 , chu2 que4 Wu1 Shan1 bu4 shi4 yun2
defs: there are no rivers to one who has crossed the ocean, and no clouds to one who has passed Mount Wu (idiom)
one who has seen the world doesn't stop at small things
Zeng Fanren, president of Shandong University from February 1998 until July 2000
Sir Donald Tsang, aka Tsang Yam-Kuen (1944–), the second Chief Executive of Hong Kong (2005–2012)
see 曾都區|曾都区[Zeng1 du1 Qu1]
Zengdu, a district of Suizhou City 隨州市|随州市[Sui2 zhou1 Shi4], Hubei
Zeng Jinyan (1983-), Chinese blogger and human rights activist, wife of dissident activist Hu Jia 胡佳[Hu2 Jia1]
Zeng Gong (1019–1083), Song dynasty writer, one of the eight giants 唐宋八大家[Tang2 Song4 ba1 da4 jia1]
